sanskrit: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः । निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंका त्याग करके स्पृहारहित, ममतारहित और अहंकाररहित होकर आचरण करता है, वह शान्तिको प्राप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: One who gives up all desires, moves without craving, without possessiveness, and without ego, attains peace.
essence: Peace begins when wanting, owning, and self-importance fall away.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who acts after releasing desire, possessiveness, and ego finds peace.
simpleInsight: There is relief in moving through life without needing to own, control, or prove anything.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna keeps refining the way to inner freedom: not by escaping action, but by acting after dropping craving, possessiveness, and ego. That is the doorway to peace.
modernRelevance: You send the message, then keep checking for a reply. You finish the work, then want credit. This verse points to the calm that comes when action is no longer chained to grasping.
translationLiteral: विहाय (giving up) / कामान् (desires) / यः (who) / सर्वान् (all) / पुमान् (person) / चरति (moves, acts) / निःस्पृहः (without craving) / निर्ममः (without possessiveness) / निरहंकारः (without ego) / सः (that one) / शांतिम् (peace) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
